note: gnode is a Group submodule providing the group_node GroupRelationType plugin (with a per-node-type deriver) to relate nodes to groups. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded); no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with the Group module, which submodule and plugin do I use to add core nodes to a group?
evals.expected_output: The gnode (Group Node) submodule provides the group_node GroupRelationType plugin, which relates nodes to groups.

evals.prompt: In Group v3 with gnode enabled, why do I see plugins like `group_node:article` and `group_node:page` rather than a single `group_node`? What creates them?
evals.expected_output: GroupNodeDeriver derives one group_node:{node_type} plugin per node type, so you install/relate content per bundle.

evals.prompt: In PHP on a Drupal 11 Group site, how do I relate an existing article node to a group using gnode?
evals.expected_output: $group->addRelationship($node, 'group_node:article'); (the plugin id is derived per node type).

evals.prompt: Which per-group permission does gnode add for viewing the overview of all of a group's nodes regardless of node type?
evals.expected_output: The `access group_node overview` per-group permission (granted via group_role), which also gates the group's Nodes operation link.

evals.prompt: With gnode's group_node plugin, how many groups can a single node belong to, and how is node access affected once a node is grouped?
evals.expected_output: Entity cardinality is 1 (a node belongs to at most one group); the plugin sets entity_access: TRUE so node access is routed through the group's per-group permissions.

evals.prompt: gnode has no settings form of its own. Where do I actually enable node content for a group type on a Drupal 11 Group site?
evals.expected_output: Install the group_node:{type} plugin on the group type via the parent Group content page /admin/group/types/manage/{group_type}/content (route entity.group_type.content_plugins).
